title: I Tried Building a Real App With AI. It Took a Year. | Alex Hyett
excerpt: My wife and I have been looking for an app to track things like habits, hobbies, and when we last did certain chores.
content_html:
17 Jul 2026

  • #ios #ai #programming

75.3k subscribers

Subscribe so you don't miss out on new videos

My wife and I have been looking for an app to track things like habits, hobbies, and when we last did certain chores.

To be honest there are probably hundreds of different habit trackers on the App Store. Next to todo list apps they are supposed to be the easiest apps to create so it is not really that surprising there are so many options. But we must have tried dozens of different apps but none of them quite fit what we were looking for.

Of course now we have AI, so this sort of thing should be easy to create. You just right a couple of prompts and AI will build it for you, while you sleep, or sip cocktails on the beach.

As we shall see it didn't quite turn out that way.

Requirements and Existing Apps #

So for this app I had a few requirements:

  1. We wanted to have the habits in a long list and not have to go through multiple pages to find them.
  2. We didn't want any data be stored in the cloud or on other peoples servers.
  3. We wanted to be able to use the gen-emojis from iOS 18 for some of the icons. It is a great option when the predefined icons don't fit.
  4. We needed to set goals on a daily, weekly, or monthly basis.
  5. It should be possible to have no goal and just count the number of occurrences. My wife sometimes gets headaches so she wanted to keep track of all of those as well.
  6. We wanted the ability to add notes. I wanted to be able to write down what exercises I did in each of my workouts and what speed I had got a song to when practicing guitar.
  7. We wanted to set reminders on habits, either every day or on specific days of the week or month.

There is nothing in this list that is particularly groundbreaking so I had a look at the various options to see if something would work to save me having to develop something.

We first tried the Streaks app. This is a completely free app on iOS and even has an Apple Health integration.

This suited most of my needs but it couldn't do some of the requirements on this list:

  • The apps were in pages instead of a long list. When you have 20 things you want to track this can get pretty tedious flicking through all the pages. This app has won design awards so that is probably more of a me issue.
  • My wife couldn't find suitable icons for some of the things she wanted to do. Hence wanting the ability to create your own.
  • There was no way to count occurrences. You had to set how many times you wanted to do something every day. Which if you are tracking headaches isn't what you want as ideally you don't want any.
  • She also realised she needed a way of turning off streaks. Streaks aren't always helpful for everything that you are tracking.

We tried a few more apps as well. There is a popular one called HabitKit and another one called Grit. I quite liked the GitHub style contribution charts of HabitKit but my wife, not being a developer wasn't really fussed by this. Grit was probably the closet to what we were looking for but still lacked a few features.

To be honest I would have been quite happy with either HabitKit or Grit for my needs if it wasn't for one thing, the price.

Now I don't mind paying for applications. I have lots of apps on my phone that I have bought as well as desktop software I have paid for. I am all for supporting indie developers but I also remember a time when an iPhone app was the price of a coffee.

Grit on the other hand costs £9.99/month or £29.99/year. There is an option to buy a lifetime licence but it costs a whopping £44.99. That is nearly the cost of a new console game.

It is the same story with HabitKit as well but not quite as much at £1.99/month or £11.99/year with a £29.99 lifetime option.

Maybe I am just a stingy git but I don't want to add even more subscriptions to my budget I have enough as it is.

So I set out to build one myself with the help of AI.

I started this project in March last year. Cursor were giving away free credits and it was the perfect opportunity to see what AI was capable of. Up until this point I had mostly avoided using AI beyond simple chat questions. Given I had never developed anything in Swift before I needed all the help I can get.

We decided on the name HabitTed and my wife even designed a cute bear logo.

AI Development Process #

If you haven't developed an iOS app before you are pretty much limited to using XCode if you want to test your application on a simulator and debug it. So there was a lot of back and forth between cursor and Xcode to try and get everything set up.

This was just before Claude Code was released so this wasn't full agentic hands-off engineering. Instead, I broke down the development into lots of small tasks and went through them manually with the AI, testing each change as I went along.

I started with a simple hello world application. Then added in the habit list, the ability to add a habit, edit a habit and so on.

To be fair after about 6 hours over the course of a weekend, I had an app that appeared to work. It didn't do everything I wanted yet but you could create a habit with a custom icon, you could click on it to complete it, and it supported various goal options and even had iCloud syncing.

Even though I had something working and only took 6 hours, I can't say it was a pleasant 6 hours.

Usually when I complete a project, I am proud of what I have accomplished. Even if I had problems and things took longer than needed, I would finish it having learned something new.

Those failures taught me something and any frustrations I had during development were usually worth it by the end and at least I learned not to make the same mistake again.

However, after 6 hours I can't say I learnt much about Swift development.

Whenever there was an error I would find myself copy and pasting the error message and asking AI to try and fix things. I was getting annoyed with the AI for not being good enough to solve the issue, but also annoyed with myself that I couldn't do it either.

The Problems #

Even though I hadn't programmed in Swift before I have programmed in many other languages and looking at the code I could tell it wasn't good code.

Each of the views was nearly a 1000 lines long. The compiler on XCode couldn't handle the length and was always telling me to break things down. Styling for things like buttons that looked the same had completely different code in different views. The whole thing was a bit of mess.

So instead of relying on AI to try and fix the mess I decided to refactor it myself, manually, the old school way. I moved everything into separate views that were no more than 100 lines long and I fixed all the inconsistent code.

I then tested the app some more to make sure everything worked properly and found even more problems.

I found that the iCloud sync didn't actually work. It may have been syncing to iCloud but if you uninstalled the application and reinstalled it, it would just lose all your data.

I also found that once I had added a lot of completions to my habits, things started to become really slow. I realised the stats I had on the details page were looping through all the habits to calculate them every single time you went on the page.

I created some properties on the habits to store the stats instead, and moved the calculation to be done on habit completion, and then I realised the data model was overly complex and had some really weird naming. This led me to coming up with a data migration plan, otherwise habits would break whenever I changed the schema.

Once everything was fixed, I added in some of the missing features such as reminders and different goal options.

Then iOS 26 came out.

I am not sure if the used AI a lot for development in Apple, but iOS 26 had a lot of bugs.

I am really not a fan of the glass effect, so I turned on the reduce transparency accessibility option and this caused all sorts of problems in my app.

I use dark mode everywhere but opening a habit caused the title bar to turn white but with white text too so you couldn't read anything.

This is where AI really falls apart. iOS 26 had only just been released so AI couldn't help with any of the new changes. I even tried to convince it that iOS 26 was the latest version and it told me I must be mistaken.

In the end I managed to solve some of my problems from reading blog posts from other developers and ultimately Apple fixed the transparency bug in 26.1.

The Result #

So after many months of development, and even more months of testing I finally have an app that I am happy enough to put my name against and the code is something I am happy to carry on maintaining.

When it comes to releasing an app there is more than just the app itself, you have to put together a website and all the screenshots as well for the app listing. This always takes a lot longer than you anticipate.

It is available on the App Store if you want to give it a try, I will leave a link in the description. It is free to use for up to 6 habits, and if you did want to support me you can unlock everything for the price of a single coffee.

If it wasn't for AI, I probably wouldn't have started this app to begin with so for that I am thankful.

On the other hand, if I hadn't have used AI at all, I would have learnt a lot more and I wouldn't have needed to spend months cleaning up the codebase and fixing all the bugs.

After 6 hours, AI produced something usable but on deeper inspection it was a mess. AI coding can get you 80% there, but the last 20% will take you 80% of the time or even longer if you don't understand what it has written.

AI is more capable now than it was a year ago, but it is also more expensive and it doesn't solve the fact that by using it your aren't gaining any skills yourself. If your not able to do your job when Claude is down then you are relying far too much on AI.

I do wonder what this industry is going to be like in a few years time. No juniors are getting hired because companies are relying on senior developers with AI to do all the work.

Meanwhile, the senior developers are in 2 camps.

One half are using AI for absolutely everything but are slowly losing their ability to code. It is like a frog in boiling water that doesn't know it's dead until it is too late.

The other half are so sick of AI and are getting burnt out having to write prompts every day that they just don't want to do it anymore.

If the AI bubble does ever pop, I am not sure there is going to be enough competent developers left who are willing to pick up the pieces. Spending your time cleaning up AI slop that you didn't want to write in the first place doesn't sound like much fun.

Whichever side you're on, just make sure you don't sit in that hot water for too long.

sentences.zh: 她还意识到她需要一种方法来关闭连续出现的情况。

sentences.en: Streaks aren't always helpful for everything that you are tracking.
sentences.zh: 条纹并不总是对您跟踪的所有内容都有帮助。

blocks.html_en: We tried a few more apps as well. There is a popular one called HabitKit and another one called Grit. I quite liked the GitHub style contribution charts of HabitKit but my wife, not being a developer wasn't really fussed by this. Grit was probably the closet to what we were looking for but still lacked a few features.

sentences.en: We tried a few more apps as well.
sentences.zh: 我们还尝试了更多应用程序。

sentences.en: There is a popular one called HabitKit and another one called Grit.
sentences.zh: 有一种流行的工具称为 HabitKit，另一种工具称为 Grit。

sentences.en: I quite liked the GitHub style contribution charts of HabitKit but my wife, not being a developer wasn't really fussed by this.
sentences.zh: 我非常喜欢 HabitKit 的 GitHub 风格的贡献图表，但我的妻子（不是开发人员）并没有对此感到大惊小怪。

sentences.en: Grit was probably the closet to what we were looking for but still lacked a few features.
sentences.zh: Grit 可能是我们正在寻找的最接近的东西，但仍然缺乏一些功能。

blocks.html_en: To be honest I would have been quite happy with either HabitKit or Grit for my needs if it wasn't for one thing, the price.

sentences.en: To be honest I would have been quite happy with either HabitKit or Grit for my needs if it wasn't for one thing, the price.
sentences.zh: 老实说，如果不是因为价格，我会很满意 HabitKit 或 Grit 来满足我的需求。

blocks.html_en: Now I don't mind paying for applications. I have lots of apps on my phone that I have bought as well as desktop software I have paid for. I am all for supporting indie developers but I also remember a time when an iPhone app was the price of a coffee.

sentences.en: Now I don't mind paying for applications.
sentences.zh: 现在我不介意支付申请费用。

sentences.en: I have lots of apps on my phone that I have bought as well as desktop software I have paid for.
sentences.zh: 我的手机上有很多我购买的应用程序以及我付费的桌面软件。

sentences.en: I am all for supporting indie developers but I also remember a time when an iPhone app was the price of a coffee.
sentences.zh: 我完全支持独立开发者，但我也记得曾经 iPhone 应用程序的价格是一杯咖啡的价格。

blocks.html_en: Grit on the other hand costs £9.99/month or £29.99/year. There is an option to buy a lifetime licence but it costs a whopping £44.99. That is nearly the cost of a new console game.

sentences.en: Grit on the other hand costs £9.99/month or £29.99/year.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，Grit 的费用为 9.99 英镑/月或 29.99 英镑/年。

sentences.en: There is an option to buy a lifetime licence but it costs a whopping £44.99.
sentences.zh: 您可以选择购买终身许可证，但价格高达 44.99 英镑。

sentences.en: That is nearly the cost of a new console game.
sentences.zh: 这几乎是一款新主机游戏的成本。

blocks.html_en: It is the same story with HabitKit as well but not quite as much at £1.99/month or £11.99/year with a £29.99 lifetime option.

sentences.en: It is the same story with HabitKit as well but not quite as much at £1.99/month or £11.99/year with a £29.99 lifetime option.
sentences.zh: HabitKit 的情况也是如此，但价格没有那么高，价格为 1.99 英镑/月或 11.99 英镑/年，终身选项为 29.99 英镑。

blocks.html_en: Maybe I am just a stingy git but I don't want to add even more subscriptions to my budget I have enough as it is.

sentences.en: Maybe I am just a stingy git but I don't want to add even more subscriptions to my budget I have enough as it is.
sentences.zh: 也许我只是一个小气的 git，但我不想在我的预算中添加更多的订阅，我已经足够了。

blocks.html_en: So I set out to build one myself with the help of AI.

sentences.en: So I set out to build one myself with the help of AI.
sentences.zh: 所以我开始在人工智能的帮助下自己构建一个。

blocks.html_en: I started this project in March last year. Cursor were giving away free credits and it was the perfect opportunity to see what AI was capable of. Up until this point I had mostly avoided using AI beyond simple chat questions. Given I had never developed anything in Swift before I needed all the help I can get.

sentences.en: I started this project in March last year.
sentences.zh: 我于去年三月开始了这个项目。

sentences.en: Cursor were giving away free credits and it was the perfect opportunity to see what AI was capable of.
sentences.zh: Cursor 正在赠送免费积分，这是了解人工智能能力的绝佳机会。

sentences.en: Up until this point I had mostly avoided using AI beyond simple chat questions.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，除了简单的聊天问题之外，我基本上避免使用人工智能。

sentences.en: Given I had never developed anything in Swift before I needed all the help I can get.
sentences.zh: 鉴于在我需要所有我能得到的帮助之前，我从未用 Swift 开发过任何东西。

blocks.html_en: We decided on the name HabitTed and my wife even designed a cute bear logo.

sentences.en: We decided on the name HabitTed and my wife even designed a cute bear logo.
sentences.zh: 我们决定取名为 HabitTed，我的妻子甚至设计了一个可爱的熊标志。

blocks.html_en: AI Development Process #

sentences.en: AI Development Process #
sentences.zh: 人工智能开发流程#

blocks.html_en: If you haven't developed an iOS app before you are pretty much limited to using XCode if you want to test your application on a simulator and debug it. So there was a lot of back and forth between cursor and Xcode to try and get everything set up.

sentences.en: If you haven't developed an iOS app before you are pretty much limited to using XCode if you want to test your application on a simulator and debug it.
sentences.zh: 如果您之前没有开发过 iOS 应用程序，那么如果您想在模拟器上测试您的应用程序并进行调试，那么您只能使用 XCode。

sentences.en: So there was a lot of back and forth between cursor and Xcode to try and get everything set up.
sentences.zh: 因此，在光标和 Xcode 之间进行了大量的来回尝试，以尝试完成所有设置。

blocks.html_en: This was just before Claude Code was released so this wasn't full agentic hands-off engineering. Instead, I broke down the development into lots of small tasks and went through them manually with the AI, testing each change as I went along.

sentences.en: This was just before Claude Code was released so this wasn't full agentic hands-off engineering.
sentences.zh: 这是在 Claude Code 发布之前，所以这并不是完全代理的不干涉工程。

sentences.en: Instead, I broke down the development into lots of small tasks and went through them manually with the AI, testing each change as I went along.
sentences.zh: 相反，我将开发分解为许多小任务，并使用人工智能手动完成它们，并在进行过程中测试每个更改。

blocks.html_en: I started with a simple hello world application. Then added in the habit list, the ability to add a habit, edit a habit and so on.

sentences.en: I started with a simple hello world application.
sentences.zh: 我从一个简单的 hello world 应用程序开始。

sentences.en: Then added in the habit list, the ability to add a habit, edit a habit and so on.
sentences.zh: 然后在习惯列表中添加了添加习惯、编辑习惯等功能。

blocks.html_en: To be fair after about 6 hours over the course of a weekend, I had an app that appeared to work. It didn't do everything I wanted yet but you could create a habit with a custom icon, you could click on it to complete it, and it supported various goal options and even had iCloud syncing.

sentences.en: To be fair after about 6 hours over the course of a weekend, I had an app that appeared to work.
sentences.zh: 公平地说，在周末大约 6 个小时后，我有了一个看起来可以运行的应用程序。

sentences.en: It didn't do everything I wanted yet but you could create a habit with a custom icon, you could click on it to complete it, and it supported various goal options and even had iCloud syncing.
sentences.zh: 它还没有完成我想要的一切，但你可以使用自定义图标创建一个习惯，你可以点击它来完成它，它支持各种目标选项，甚至有 iCloud 同步。

blocks.html_en: Even though I had something working and only took 6 hours, I can't say it was a pleasant 6 hours.

sentences.en: Even though I had something working and only took 6 hours, I can't say it was a pleasant 6 hours.
sentences.zh: 尽管我有一些工作要做，只花了 6 个小时，但我不能说这是一个愉快的 6 个小时。

blocks.html_en: Usually when I complete a project, I am proud of what I have accomplished. Even if I had problems and things took longer than needed, I would finish it having learned something new.

sentences.en: Usually when I complete a project, I am proud of what I have accomplished.
sentences.zh: 通常，当我完成一个项目时，我会为自己所取得的成就感到自豪。

sentences.en: Even if I had problems and things took longer than needed, I would finish it having learned something new.
sentences.zh: 即使我遇到问题并且事情花费的时间比需要的时间长，我也会在学到新东西的情况下完成它。

blocks.html_en: Those failures taught me something and any frustrations I had during development were usually worth it by the end and at least I learned not to make the same mistake again.

sentences.en: Those failures taught me something and any frustrations I had during development were usually worth it by the end and at least I learned not to make the same mistake again.
sentences.zh: 这些失败教会了我一些东西，在开发过程中遇到的任何挫折通常到最后都是值得的，至少我学会了不再犯同样的错误。

blocks.html_en: However, after 6 hours I can't say I learnt much about Swift development.

sentences.en: However, after 6 hours I can't say I learnt much about Swift development.
sentences.zh: 然而，6 个小时之后，我并不能说我学到了很多关于 Swift 开发的知识。

blocks.html_en: Whenever there was an error I would find myself copy and pasting the error message and asking AI to try and fix things. I was getting annoyed with the AI for not being good enough to solve the issue, but also annoyed with myself that I couldn't do it either.

sentences.en: Whenever there was an error I would find myself copy and pasting the error message and asking AI to try and fix things.
sentences.zh: 每当出现错误时，我都会发现自己复制并粘贴错误消息，并要求人工智能尝试修复问题。

sentences.en: I was getting annoyed with the AI for not being good enough to solve the issue, but also annoyed with myself that I couldn't do it either.
sentences.zh: 我对人工智能没有足够好来解决问题感到恼火，但也对自己无法做到这一点感到恼火。

blocks.html_en: The Problems #

sentences.en: The Problems #
sentences.zh: 问题#

blocks.html_en: Even though I hadn't programmed in Swift before I have programmed in many other languages and looking at the code I could tell it wasn't good code.

sentences.en: Even though I hadn't programmed in Swift before I have programmed in many other languages and looking at the code I could tell it wasn't good code.
sentences.zh: 尽管我在使用许多其他语言进行编程之前从未使用过 Swift 编程，但通过查看代码，我可以看出它不是好代码。

blocks.html_en: Each of the views was nearly a 1000 lines long. The compiler on XCode couldn't handle the length and was always telling me to break things down. Styling for things like buttons that looked the same had completely different code in different views. The whole thing was a bit of mess.

sentences.en: Each of the views was nearly a 1000 lines long.
sentences.zh: 每个视图都有近 1000 行长。

sentences.en: The compiler on XCode couldn't handle the length and was always telling me to break things down.
sentences.zh: XCode 上的编译器无法处理这个长度，并且总是告诉我将其分解。

sentences.en: Styling for things like buttons that looked the same had completely different code in different views.
sentences.zh: 像按钮这样看起来相同的东西的样式在不同的视图中具有完全不同的代码。

sentences.en: The whole thing was a bit of mess.
sentences.zh: 整个事情有点混乱。

blocks.html_en: So instead of relying on AI to try and fix the mess I decided to refactor it myself, manually, the old school way. I moved everything into separate views that were no more than 100 lines long and I fixed all the inconsistent code.

sentences.en: So instead of relying on AI to try and fix the mess I decided to refactor it myself, manually, the old school way.
sentences.zh: 因此，我决定自己以传统的方式手动重构它，而不是依靠人工智能来尝试解决这个问题。

sentences.en: I moved everything into separate views that were no more than 100 lines long and I fixed all the inconsistent code.
sentences.zh: 我将所有内容移至长度不超过 100 行的单独视图中，并修复了所有不一致的代码。

blocks.html_en: I then tested the app some more to make sure everything worked properly and found even more problems.

sentences.en: I then tested the app some more to make sure everything worked properly and found even more problems.
sentences.zh: 然后我对应用程序进行了更多测试，以确保一切正常，并发现了更多问题。

blocks.html_en: I found that the iCloud sync didn't actually work. It may have been syncing to iCloud but if you uninstalled the application and reinstalled it, it would just lose all your data.

sentences.en: I found that the iCloud sync didn't actually work.
sentences.zh: 我发现 iCloud 同步实际上不起作用。

sentences.en: It may have been syncing to iCloud but if you uninstalled the application and reinstalled it, it would just lose all your data.
sentences.zh: 它可能已同步到 iCloud，但如果您卸载该应用程序并重新安装，它只会丢失所有数据。

blocks.html_en: I also found that once I had added a lot of completions to my habits, things started to become really slow. I realised the stats I had on the details page were looping through all the habits to calculate them every single time you went on the page.

sentences.en: I also found that once I had added a lot of completions to my habits, things started to become really slow.
sentences.zh: 我还发现，一旦我在习惯中添加了很多完成项，事情就开始变得非常缓慢。

sentences.en: I realised the stats I had on the details page were looping through all the habits to calculate them every single time you went on the page.
sentences.zh: 我意识到详细信息页面上的统计数据正在循环遍历所有习惯，以便在您每次访问该页面时计算它们。

blocks.html_en: I created some properties on the habits to store the stats instead, and moved the calculation to be done on habit completion, and then I realised the data model was overly complex and had some really weird naming. This led me to coming up with a data migration plan, otherwise habits would break whenever I changed the schema.

sentences.en: I created some properties on the habits to store the stats instead, and moved the calculation to be done on habit completion, and then I realised the data model was overly complex and had some really weird naming.
sentences.zh: 我在习惯上创建了一些属性来存储统计数据，并将计算移至习惯完成时完成，然后我意识到数据模型过于复杂，并且有一些非常奇怪的命名。

sentences.en: This led me to coming up with a data migration plan, otherwise habits would break whenever I changed the schema.
sentences.zh: 这促使我制定了一个数据迁移计划，否则每当我更改架构时习惯就会被打破。

blocks.html_en: Once everything was fixed, I added in some of the missing features such as reminders and different goal options.

sentences.en: Once everything was fixed, I added in some of the missing features such as reminders and different goal options.
sentences.zh: 一旦一切都解决了，我添加了一些缺失的功能，例如提醒和不同的目标选项。

blocks.html_en: Then iOS 26 came out.

sentences.en: Then iOS 26 came out.
sentences.zh: 然后iOS 26出来了。

blocks.html_en: I am not sure if the used AI a lot for development in Apple, but iOS 26 had a lot of bugs.

sentences.en: I am not sure if the used AI a lot for development in Apple, but iOS 26 had a lot of bugs.
sentences.zh: 我不确定苹果是否大量使用人工智能进行开发，但 iOS 26 有很多错误。

blocks.html_en: I am really not a fan of the glass effect, so I turned on the reduce transparency accessibility option and this caused all sorts of problems in my app.

sentences.en: I am really not a fan of the glass effect, so I turned on the reduce transparency accessibility option and this caused all sorts of problems in my app.
sentences.zh: 我真的不喜欢玻璃效果，所以我打开了降低透明度辅助功能选项，这导致了我的应用程序中的各种问题。

blocks.html_en: I use dark mode everywhere but opening a habit caused the title bar to turn white but with white text too so you couldn't read anything.

sentences.en: I use dark mode everywhere but opening a habit caused the title bar to turn white but with white text too so you couldn't read anything.
sentences.zh: 我到处都使用深色模式，但打开一个习惯会导致标题栏变成白色，但也带有白色文本，因此您无法阅读任何内容。

blocks.html_en: This is where AI really falls apart. iOS 26 had only just been released so AI couldn't help with any of the new changes. I even tried to convince it that iOS 26 was the latest version and it told me I must be mistaken.

sentences.en: This is where AI really falls apart.
sentences.zh: 这就是人工智能真正崩溃的地方。

sentences.en: iOS 26 had only just been released so AI couldn't help with any of the new changes.
sentences.zh: iOS 26 刚刚发布，因此 AI 无法帮助解决任何新变化。

sentences.en: I even tried to convince it that iOS 26 was the latest version and it told me I must be mistaken.
sentences.zh: 我什至试图说服它 iOS 26 是最新版本，但它告诉我我一定是弄错了。

blocks.html_en: In the end I managed to solve some of my problems from reading blog posts from other developers and ultimately Apple fixed the transparency bug in 26.1.

sentences.en: In the end I managed to solve some of my problems from reading blog posts from other developers and ultimately Apple fixed the transparency bug in 26.1.
sentences.zh: 最后，我通过阅读其他开发者的博客文章成功解决了一些问题，最终苹果在 26.1 中修复了透明度错误。

blocks.html_en: The Result #

sentences.en: The Result #
sentences.zh: 结果#

blocks.html_en: So after many months of development, and even more months of testing I finally have an app that I am happy enough to put my name against and the code is something I am happy to carry on maintaining.

sentences.en: So after many months of development, and even more months of testing I finally have an app that I am happy enough to put my name against and the code is something I am happy to carry on maintaining.
sentences.zh: 因此，经过几个月的开发，甚至更多个月的测试，我终于有了一个应用程序，我很高兴能在其中写下我的名字，并且我很乐意继续维护代码。

blocks.html_en: When it comes to releasing an app there is more than just the app itself, you have to put together a website and all the screenshots as well for the app listing. This always takes a lot longer than you anticipate.

sentences.en: When it comes to releasing an app there is more than just the app itself, you have to put together a website and all the screenshots as well for the app listing.
sentences.zh: 在发布应用程序时，不仅仅是应用程序本身，您还必须将网站和所有屏幕截图以及应用程序列表放在一起。

sentences.en: This always takes a lot longer than you anticipate.
sentences.zh: 这总是比您预期的时间要长得多。

blocks.html_en: It is available on the App Store if you want to give it a try, I will leave a link in the description. It is free to use for up to 6 habits, and if you did want to support me you can unlock everything for the price of a single coffee.

sentences.en: It is available on the App Store if you want to give it a try, I will leave a link in the description.
sentences.zh: 如果你想尝试一下，它可以在 App Store 上找到，我会在描述中留下一个链接。

sentences.en: It is free to use for up to 6 habits, and if you did want to support me you can unlock everything for the price of a single coffee.
sentences.zh: 它可以免费用于最多 6 个习惯，如果您确实想支持我，您只需一杯咖啡的价格即可解锁所有内容。

blocks.html_en: If it wasn't for AI, I probably wouldn't have started this app to begin with so for that I am thankful.

sentences.en: If it wasn't for AI, I probably wouldn't have started this app to begin with so for that I am thankful.
sentences.zh: 如果没有人工智能，我可能不会开始这个应用程序，所以我很感激。

blocks.html_en: On the other hand, if I hadn't have used AI at all, I would have learnt a lot more and I wouldn't have needed to spend months cleaning up the codebase and fixing all the bugs.

sentences.en: On the other hand, if I hadn't have used AI at all, I would have learnt a lot more and I wouldn't have needed to spend months cleaning up the codebase and fixing all the bugs.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，如果我根本没有使用人工智能，我会学到更多东西，我就不需要花几个月的时间清理代码库并修复所有错误。

blocks.html_en: After 6 hours, AI produced something usable but on deeper inspection it was a mess. AI coding can get you 80% there, but the last 20% will take you 80% of the time or even longer if you don't understand what it has written.

sentences.en: After 6 hours, AI produced something usable but on deeper inspection it was a mess.
sentences.zh: 6 小时后，人工智能产生了一些可用的东西，但经过更深入的检查，结果却是一团糟。

sentences.en: AI coding can get you 80% there, but the last 20% will take you 80% of the time or even longer if you don't understand what it has written.
sentences.zh: AI 编码可以让你完成 80% 的任务，但如果你不理解它写的内容，那么最后 20% 的任务将花费你 80% 甚至更长的时间。

blocks.html_en: AI is more capable now than it was a year ago, but it is also more expensive and it doesn't solve the fact that by using it your aren't gaining any skills yourself. If your not able to do your job when Claude is down then you are relying far too much on AI.

sentences.en: AI is more capable now than it was a year ago, but it is also more expensive and it doesn't solve the fact that by using it your aren't gaining any skills yourself.
sentences.zh: 人工智能现在比一年前更有能力，但它也更昂贵，而且它并没有解决通过使用它你自己无法获得任何技能的事实。

sentences.en: If your not able to do your job when Claude is down then you are relying far too much on AI.
sentences.zh: 如果你在克劳德情绪低落时无法完成你的工作，那么你就过于依赖人工智能了。

blocks.html_en: I do wonder what this industry is going to be like in a few years time. No juniors are getting hired because companies are relying on senior developers with AI to do all the work.

sentences.en: I do wonder what this industry is going to be like in a few years time.
sentences.zh: 我确实想知道几年后这个行业会是什么样子。

sentences.en: No juniors are getting hired because companies are relying on senior developers with AI to do all the work.
sentences.zh: 没有初级员工被雇用，因为公司依靠人工智能的高级开发人员来完成所有工作。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, the senior developers are in 2 camps.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, the senior developers are in 2 camps.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，高级开发人员分为两个阵营。

blocks.html_en: One half are using AI for absolutely everything but are slowly losing their ability to code. It is like a frog in boiling water that doesn't know it's dead until it is too late.

sentences.en: One half are using AI for absolutely everything but are slowly losing their ability to code.
sentences.zh: 一半人在所有事情上都使用人工智能，但正在慢慢失去编码能力。

sentences.en: It is like a frog in boiling water that doesn't know it's dead until it is too late.
sentences.zh: 就像沸水中的青蛙，直到为时已晚才知道自己已经死了。

blocks.html_en: The other half are so sick of AI and are getting burnt out having to write prompts every day that they just don't want to do it anymore.

sentences.en: The other half are so sick of AI and are getting burnt out having to write prompts every day that they just don't want to do it anymore.
sentences.zh: 另一半人已经厌倦了人工智能，每天都必须写提示，他们已经精疲力尽，他们只是不想再这样做了。

blocks.html_en: If the AI bubble does ever pop, I am not sure there is going to be enough competent developers left who are willing to pick up the pieces. Spending your time cleaning up AI slop that you didn't want to write in the first place doesn't sound like much fun.

sentences.en: If the AI bubble does ever pop, I am not sure there is going to be enough competent developers left who are willing to pick up the pieces.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能泡沫真的破灭，我不确定是否还会有足够多有能力的开发人员愿意收拾残局。

sentences.en: Spending your time cleaning up AI slop that you didn't want to write in the first place doesn't sound like much fun.
sentences.zh: 花时间清理你本来不想写的 AI 垃圾听起来并不有趣。

blocks.html_en: Whichever side you're on, just make sure you don't sit in that hot water for too long.

sentences.en: Whichever side you're on, just make sure you don't sit in that hot water for too long.
sentences.zh: 无论您站在哪一边，只要确保不要在热水中坐太久即可。

content_html_zh:
17 Jul 2026

  • #ios #ai #编程

75,300 订阅者

订阅，这样您就不会错过新视频

我和我的妻子一直在寻找一款应用程序来跟踪习惯、爱好以及我们上次做某些家务的时间。

老实说，App Store 上可能有数百种不同的习惯追踪器。 除了待办事项列表应用程序之外，它们应该是最容易创建的应用程序，因此有这么多选项也就不足为奇了。 但我们一定尝试过数十种不同的应用程序，但没有一个完全符合我们的需求。

当然现在我们有了人工智能，所以这种东西应该很容易创建。 你只需正确几个提示，人工智能就会在你睡觉或在海滩上喝鸡尾酒时为你构建它。

正如我们将看到的，事实并非如此。

要求和现有应用程序#

所以对于这个应用程序我有一些要求：

  1. 我们希望将这些习惯列在一个长列表中，而不必翻阅多个页面才能找到它们。
  2. 我们不希望任何数据存储在云端或其他人的服务器上。
  3. 我们希望能够将 iOS 18 中的 gen-emojis 用于某些图标。 当预定义图标不适合时，这是一个不错的选择。
  4. 我们需要每天、每周或每月设定目标。
  5. 应该可以没有目标，只计算出现的次数。 我的妻子有时会头痛，所以她也想记录所有这些情况。
  6. 我们希望能够添加注释。 我希望能够写下我在每次锻炼中所做的练习以及练习吉他时我的歌曲速度。
  7. 我们希望为习惯设置提醒，可以是每天，也可以是一周或一个月的特定日期。

这个列表中没有什么特别具有开创性的，所以我查看了各种选项，看看是否有一些东西可以让我不必开发一些东西。

我们首先尝试了 Streaks 应用程序。 这是 iOS 上完全免费的应用程序，甚至还集成了 Apple Health。

这满足了我的大部分需求，但它无法满足此列表中的一些要求：

  • 这些应用程序位于页面中，而不是一长串列表中。 当您想要跟踪 20 件事情时，浏览所有页面可能会变得非常乏味。 这个应用程序赢得了设计奖项，所以这可能更多是我的问题。
  • 我的妻子无法为她想做的一些事情找到合适的图标。 因此希望有能力创建自己的。
  • 没有办法计算发生次数。 你必须设定每天想做某件事的次数。 如果你正在追踪头痛，这不是你想要的，因为理想情况下你不想要任何。
  • 她还意识到她需要一种方法来关闭连续出现的情况。 条纹并不总是对您跟踪的所有内容都有帮助。

我们还尝试了更多应用程序。 有一种流行的工具称为 HabitKit，另一种工具称为 Grit。 我非常喜欢 HabitKit 的 GitHub 风格的贡献图表，但我的妻子（不是开发人员）并没有对此感到大惊小怪。 Grit 可能是我们正在寻找的最接近的东西，但仍然缺乏一些功能。

老实说，如果不是因为价格，我会很满意 HabitKit 或 Grit 来满足我的需求。

现在我不介意支付申请费用。 我的手机上有很多我购买的应用程序以及我付费的桌面软件。 我完全支持独立开发者，但我也记得曾经 iPhone 应用程序的价格是一杯咖啡的价格。

另一方面，Grit 的费用为 9.99 英镑/月或 29.99 英镑/年。 您可以选择购买终身许可证，但价格高达 44.99 英镑。 这几乎是一款新主机游戏的成本。

HabitKit 的情况也是如此，但价格没有那么高，价格为 1.99 英镑/月或 11.99 英镑/年，终身选项为 29.99 英镑。

也许我只是一个小气的 git，但我不想在我的预算中添加更多的订阅，我已经足够了。

所以我开始在人工智能的帮助下自己构建一个。

我于去年三月开始了这个项目。 Cursor 正在赠送免费积分，这是了解人工智能能力的绝佳机会。 到目前为止，除了简单的聊天问题之外，我基本上避免使用人工智能。 鉴于在我需要所有我能得到的帮助之前，我从未用 Swift 开发过任何东西。

我们决定取名为 HabitTed，我的妻子甚至设计了一个可爱的熊标志。

人工智能开发流程#

如果您之前没有开发过 iOS 应用程序，那么如果您想在模拟器上测试您的应用程序并进行调试，那么您只能使用 XCode。 因此，在光标和 Xcode 之间进行了大量的来回尝试，以尝试完成所有设置。

这是在 Claude Code 发布之前，所以这并不是完全代理的不干涉工程。 相反，我将开发分解为许多小任务，并使用人工智能手动完成它们，并在进行过程中测试每个更改。

我从一个简单的 hello world 应用程序开始。 然后在习惯列表中添加了添加习惯、编辑习惯等功能。

公平地说，在周末大约 6 个小时后，我有了一个看起来可以运行的应用程序。 它还没有完成我想要的一切，但你可以使用自定义图标创建一个习惯，你可以点击它来完成它，它支持各种目标选项，甚至有 iCloud 同步。

尽管我有一些工作要做，只花了 6 个小时，但我不能说这是一个愉快的 6 个小时。

通常，当我完成一个项目时，我会为自己所取得的成就感到自豪。 即使我遇到问题并且事情花费的时间比需要的时间长，我也会在学到新东西的情况下完成它。

这些失败教会了我一些东西，在开发过程中遇到的任何挫折通常到最后都是值得的，至少我学会了不再犯同样的错误。

然而，6 个小时之后，我并不能说我学到了很多关于 Swift 开发的知识。

每当出现错误时，我都会发现自己复制并粘贴错误消息，并要求人工智能尝试修复问题。 我对人工智能没有足够好来解决问题感到恼火，但也对自己无法做到这一点感到恼火。

问题#

尽管我在使用许多其他语言进行编程之前从未使用过 Swift 编程，但通过查看代码，我可以看出它不是好代码。

每个视图都有近 1000 行长。 XCode 上的编译器无法处理这个长度，并且总是告诉我将其分解。 像按钮这样看起来相同的东西的样式在不同的视图中具有完全不同的代码。 整个事情有点混乱。

因此，我决定自己以传统的方式手动重构它，而不是依靠人工智能来尝试解决这个问题。 我将所有内容移至长度不超过 100 行的单独视图中，并修复了所有不一致的代码。

然后我对应用程序进行了更多测试，以确保一切正常，并发现了更多问题。

我发现 iCloud 同步实际上不起作用。 它可能已同步到 iCloud，但如果您卸载该应用程序并重新安装，它只会丢失所有数据。

我还发现，一旦我在习惯中添加了很多完成项，事情就开始变得非常缓慢。 我意识到详细信息页面上的统计数据正在循环遍历所有习惯，以便在您每次访问该页面时计算它们。

我在习惯上创建了一些属性来存储统计数据，并将计算移至习惯完成时完成，然后我意识到数据模型过于复杂，并且有一些非常奇怪的命名。 这促使我制定了一个数据迁移计划，否则每当我更改架构时习惯就会被打破。

一旦一切都解决了，我添加了一些缺失的功能，例如提醒和不同的目标选项。

然后iOS 26出来了。

我不确定苹果是否大量使用人工智能进行开发，但 iOS 26 有很多错误。

我真的不喜欢玻璃效果，所以我打开了降低透明度辅助功能选项，这导致了我的应用程序中的各种问题。

我到处都使用深色模式，但打开一个习惯会导致标题栏变成白色，但也带有白色文本，因此您无法阅读任何内容。

这就是人工智能真正崩溃的地方。 iOS 26 刚刚发布，因此 AI 无法帮助解决任何新变化。 我什至试图说服它 iOS 26 是最新版本，但它告诉我我一定是弄错了。

最后，我通过阅读其他开发者的博客文章成功解决了一些问题，最终苹果在 26.1 中修复了透明度错误。

结果#

因此，经过几个月的开发，甚至更多个月的测试，我终于有了一个应用程序，我很高兴能在其中写下我的名字，并且我很乐意继续维护代码。

在发布应用程序时，不仅仅是应用程序本身，您还必须将网站和所有屏幕截图以及应用程序列表放在一起。 这总是比您预期的时间要长得多。

如果你想尝试一下，它可以在 App Store 上找到，我会在描述中留下一个链接。 它可以免费用于最多 6 个习惯，如果您确实想支持我，您只需一杯咖啡的价格即可解锁所有内容。

如果没有人工智能，我可能不会开始这个应用程序，所以我很感激。

另一方面，如果我根本没有使用人工智能，我会学到更多东西，我就不需要花几个月的时间清理代码库并修复所有错误。

6 小时后，人工智能产生了一些可用的东西，但经过更深入的检查，结果却是一团糟。 AI 编码可以让你完成 80% 的任务，但如果你不理解它写的内容，那么最后 20% 的任务将花费你 80% 甚至更长的时间。

人工智能现在比一年前更有能力，但它也更昂贵，而且它并没有解决通过使用它你自己无法获得任何技能的事实。 如果你在克劳德情绪低落时无法完成你的工作，那么你就过于依赖人工智能了。

我确实想知道几年后这个行业会是什么样子。 没有初级员工被雇用，因为公司依靠人工智能的高级开发人员来完成所有工作。

与此同时，高级开发人员分为两个阵营。

一半人在所有事情上都使用人工智能，但正在慢慢失去编码能力。 就像沸水中的青蛙，直到为时已晚才知道自己已经死了。

另一半人已经厌倦了人工智能，每天都必须写提示，他们已经精疲力尽，他们只是不想再这样做了。

如果人工智能泡沫真的破灭，我不确定是否还会有足够多有能力的开发人员愿意收拾残局。 花时间清理你本来不想写的 AI 垃圾听起来并不有趣。

无论您站在哪一边，只要确保不要在热水中坐太久即可。

想与这篇文章互动吗？ 该网站支持网络提及，因此您可以通过从您的网站发送网络提及来点赞、评论或转发。 您也可以在 Bubbles 上为这篇文章点赞。

您还可以通过在 Mastodon 上点赞和评论这篇文章来与这篇文章互动。 只需复制已复制的帖子 URL 并将其粘贴到您的 Mastodon 搜索中即可找到它。 出于隐私原因，Mastodon 的评论未显示在下面。
